user_query:
Please set up a weekly digest generator for my travel drafts and run it once to produce a preview output.

Inputs (read-only):
- input/drafts/*.md (two sample drafts included)
- input/newsletter_intro.txt (a rough intro I wrote)

What I need:
1) A processor that reads the drafts and prepares cleaned copies plus a concise weekly newsletter preview:
- For each Markdown draft in input/drafts:
  - Extract the title from the first line that starts with "# ".
  - Find the "## Highlights" section and collect its bullet items (lines starting with "- ") until the next section or end of file.
  - Rewrite the opening paragraph (the text between the title line and the first section header like "## ") to improve clarity and brevity: 40–80 words, maximum two sentences, warm and helpful tone.
  - Save a cleaned version to output/cleaned_drafts/{original_basename}.md where ONLY the opening paragraph is replaced with your rewrite; keep the title and all subsequent sections (including Highlights bullets and anything after) unchanged.
  - Append a single line at the very end of the cleaned file:
    Summary: <first three highlight items as plain phrases separated by "; ">  (if fewer than three exist, include all)
- Build a newsletter preview at output/newsletters/weekly_preview.md with:
  - A concise rewrite of input/newsletter_intro.txt (60–90 words, 2–3 sentences, friendly and to the point).
  - Then a list with one bullet per draft, in any stable order:
    * {Title}: {first three highlight items joined by " • "}
  - No additional commentary beyond the intro and the bullet list.
- Create output/run_summary.json containing:
  {
    "processed_drafts": [list of basenames],
    "cleaned_drafts_count": <int>,
    "newsletter_preview": "output/newsletters/weekly_preview.md",
    "per_draft": {
      "<basename>": {
        "intro_word_count": <int>,
        "highlights_used": [first up to 3 highlight strings]
      }
    }
  }

2) Scheduling artifacts so this can run automatically every Monday at 09:00 Turkey time:
- scripts/run_weekly_digest.sh that runs the processor and writes/rotates a log at output/logs/weekly.log. On scheduled runs, it should create a dated newsletter file at output/newsletters/weekly_YYYYMMDD.md in the same structure as the preview (using the run date).
- config/cron.weekly_digest containing a single crontab line that runs the shell script every Monday at 09:00 with Turkey time (use TZ=Europe/Istanbul in the entry) and redirects stdout/stderr to output/logs/weekly.log. Do not install it—just provide the file.

Constraints that must be met:
- Rewritten opening paragraphs: 40–80 words, maximum two sentences.
- Rewritten newsletter intro: 60–90 words, 2–3 sentences.
- For summaries, use exactly the first three highlight bullet texts in order (or all if fewer than three).
- Preserve the original Highlights bullets and all other sections in cleaned drafts, except for replacing only the opening paragraph.
- Deterministic processing (no randomness, no external APIs or web calls). Use only files under input/.

Deliverables to create in this workspace:
- tools/weekly_digest.(py or sh), plus any helper code you need
- scripts/run_weekly_digest.sh
- config/cron.weekly_digest
- output/cleaned_drafts/*.md (for each input draft)
- output/newsletters/weekly_preview.md
- output/run_summary.json
- output/logs/weekly.log (from your first run)

Please implement and run it once now to produce the preview outputs using the provided inputs. I’m a retired Istanbul-based travel blogger; I want this to keep my tone warm but concise, and help me compile a weekly digest from my drafts without touching the web. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
